Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First let me understand the normative process 1 Always starts with Receive order 2 Next must be Check inventory before any payment authorization or fulfillment decisions 3 If inventory sufficient then Authorize payment Package items after authorization before shipping Ship order after packaging Close order final activity Sequence Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close 4 If inventory insufficient then Cancel order after checking inventory Close order final activity Sequence Receive Check Cancel Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key constraints mentioned Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Close order should be the final activity in all scenarios No shipping after cancellation No payment authorization before checking inventory No packaging before authorization

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order This follows the sufficient inventory path perfectly No violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Here Package items occurs before Authorize payment This violates the rule that packaging should only occur after payment authorization

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the sequence should be Check Authorize Package Ship But we have Check Package Authorize Ship

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order Here we have Cancel order and then Ship order after cancellation and no Package items activity at all

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So multiple violations 1 Shipping after cancellation explicitly forbidden 2 Shipping without packaging violation of Ship order should only occur after packaging 3 The sequence suggests a mixed path it started as fulfillment but then switched to cancellation but still shipped

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: This could be interpreted as they checked inventory found it sufficient so authorized payment but then something happened and they had to cancel but then shipping after cancellation is definitely wrong

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order This follows the insufficient inventory path Receive Check Cancel Close No violations here

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Missing Package items between Authorize and Ship

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The normative description states Once the payment is authorized the items should be prepared for shipment Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order So shipping without packaging is a violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Summary of violations Case 2 Packaging before payment authorization wrong order Case 3 Shipping after cancellation and shipping without packaging Case 5 Shipping without packaging skipping packaging step

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Ambiguities to consider Is it possible to authorize payment even if inventory is insufficient The text implies that if inventory is insufficient we do not authorize payment but the main clear violation in Case 3 is shipping after cancellation and lack of packaging

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I should also mention that Case 1 and 4 are compliant Case 1 follows the full successful path Case 4 follows the cancellation path
